rule: raw:m3-p5-a6-external-anchor-spec-first-omega-cycle (great-grandchild of 2026-04-25_meta_engine_final_form_and_limits, child of 2026-04-26_m3_p5_tarski_verifier_omega_cycle, narrowed to a6 external_timestamp_trust_anchor)
subject: spec/m3_external_anchor_workflow.md + spec/m3_anchor_log_schema.json + spec/m3_anchor_falsifier.md — defines git-tag + detached-signature publication of meta_decision_cert.jsonl chain_head_hash. Truncates the Tarski meta-verifier regress at V_1 by epistemic decree (Münchhausen 'dogmatic stop' at the external anchor).
purpose: Spec-first lock: workflow steps, anchor-log schema, raw 71 falsifier all written BEFORE any tooling lands. Honest documentation of trust assumptions (5+ items) including SHA-1-in-git limitation as practical-only (not theorem-strong) and key-compromise rotation contingency.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 'spec-first ω-cycle for m3.p5 external trust anchor — git tag + detached signature workflow' — parent p5 cycle marked a6 Tier-1 with theory_anchor=Münchhausen but no concrete protocol; this cycle writes that protocol.
method: spec-first triple: workflow.md (≤80 lines, 5-step protocol + 6-item trust list + verification + rotation + key-compromise) + log_schema.json (JSON Schema draft-07 for state/meta_cert_anchor_log.jsonl rows) + falsifier.md (≤30 lines, F1..F5 conditions). Each file sha256-anchored in this witness. ≤4 axes. omega_stop = three artifacts present + sha256 stable + line budgets respected + trust list ≥5 items + SHA-1 limitation explicit + key rotation procedure explicit.
parent_axis_under_deepening: a6 external_timestamp_trust_anchor (Tier-1, 15 LoC + git tag setup)
ordinal_stamp_rationale: Inherits parent (m3-p5-tarski-verifier) stamp ω·2+2 unchanged. The anchor is the EPISTEMIC TERMINATOR of the Tarski meta-verifier regress at this ordinal — it does NOT add a meta-language level (which would be ω·2+3). It instead breaks the chain by appeal to an out-of-band trust root (git remote + signed tag), so the ordinal stays at ω·2+2 by design. b1/b2/b3 of parent cycle explicitly forbid formal ascent; this spec records the dogmatic stop.

sections: 4. Rotation policy — every 10 rounds OR every 24h
5. Key-compromise contingency — stop, rotate, republish, mark untrusted

deliverables.schema: JSON Schema draft-07; required fields: ts, chain_head_hash, tag_name, signed_by_keyid, anchored_round_range{from_round,to_round}, verification_command, schema_version; optional: notes (used for key-rotation cross-references)

falsifier_clauses: F1 signature failure (git tag -v non-zero / wrong keyid / revoked)
F3 chain divergence (recomputed chain head ≠ row's chain_head_hash)
F4 missing tag (absent after git fetch --tags)
F5 rotation breach (>10 rounds AND >24h unanchored)

rotation_policy_verbatim: Anchor every 10 cert rounds OR every 24 hours, whichever comes first.
trust_assumption_list: 1. Local SSH/PGP signing key — operator's private key uncompromised; passphrase-protected; not exfiltrated. (Compromise → §5 rotation.)
2. GitHub server (or chosen remote) — receives push, records server-side receipt time, does not collude with operator to backdate. Third-party clock.
3. Git transport protocol — TLS to remote intact; no MITM rewriting tag content in flight.

4. Git's internal hashing (SHA-1) — git uses SHA-1 for object IDs as of 2026; SHA-1 is collision-broken (Stevens 2017 SHAttered, Leurent–Peyrin 2020 chosen-prefix); practical exploitation against tag objects non-trivial but NOT theorem-strong; SHA-256 git transition (extensions.objectFormat=sha256) ongoing; honest limitation — anchor only as strong as git's hash agility.
5. SHA-256 of cert log content — chain_head_hash uses SHA-256, independent of git's SHA-1; collision resistance currently sound.
6. Public observability — at least one third-party (besides operator) periodically fetches tags; without an observer, trust degrades to operator-self-trust.

axes_surfaced.name: workflow_step_minimality
axes_surfaced.summary: 5 steps (compute → name → tag -a -s → push → log) is the minimum self-contained protocol. Removing any step breaks either content-binding (2,3), public timestamping (4), or audit trail (5). Step 1 is presupposed by m3.p3 hash chain — anchor reads, never recomputes from scratch. Falsifier: any step-removal proposal that still produces verifiable anchor → spec falsified.

axes_surfaced.name: trust_list_completeness
axes_surfaced.summary: 6 items (signing key, remote, transport, git SHA-1, content SHA-256, public observability) cover the full trust surface from operator through transport through hash through observer. SHA-1 entry is the load-bearing honesty disclosure — practical-only, not theorem-strong, with explicit citation (SHAttered 2017, chosen-prefix 2020) and migration path (SHA-256 git). Falsifier: any plausible attack vector NOT covered by these 6 → list incomplete.

axes_surfaced.name: rotation_policy_choice
axes_surfaced.summary: Disjunctive 'every 10 rounds OR every 24h, whichever first' chosen over conjunctive ('every 10 rounds AND ≥1h apart') because (a) low-activity periods would otherwise stay unanchored indefinitely, (b) high-activity bursts would otherwise pile too many rounds under one anchor (blast-radius). Disjunction bounds both stale-time and unanchored-round-count. Falsifier (F5): chain head with round R > max(to_round) AND now - latest ts > 24h AND R - latest to_round ≥ 10 simultaneously.

axes_surfaced.name: key_compromise_recoverability
axes_surfaced.summary: 4-step rotation (stop → new keypair → republish from last good → mark old untrusted) preserves append-only history while restoring trust. NO history rewrite. The 'last good anchor' definition is operationally fuzzy (operator must judge), recorded in `notes` field of the rotation row. Falsifier: any rotation procedure that requires rewriting state/meta_cert_anchor_log.jsonl or deleting old git tags → violates raw 77 audit-append-only.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: Once spec is locked here, a thin shell script (~20 LoC) automates steps 1-5 idempotently. Spec-first means impl follows the recorded steps verbatim.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: Running the script twice on identical chain head must produce identical tag (modulo timestamp); push of an already-pushed tag must be a no-op.

tier_2_backlog.blocker: OpenTimestamps Bitcoin-anchor adds ~60s latency; only justified if GitHub remote becomes untrusted. Two-of-three (git remote + signed tag + OTS) for Byzantine resilience deferred.

tier_3_defer_or_split.blocker: M-of-N signing quorum (e.g., 2-of-3 maintainer keys) only justified once single-operator model demonstrates compromise risk. Single-key operation is the baseline for ω·2+2.

limit_axes_documented.label: SHA-1 collision in git internals
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: Tag objects are git-hashed with SHA-1; a sufficiently resourced attacker could craft a colliding tag object
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: acknowledged in trust assumption #4; migration to SHA-256 git ongoing; chain_head_hash itself uses SHA-256 independently of git's hash

limit_axes_documented.label: Tarski hierarchy non-termination (inherited from parent b2)
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: No internal verifier chain ever terminates; this anchor is NOT a formal terminator
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: Münchhausen dogmatic stop at external anchor — explicitly NOT a proof

limit_axes_documented.label: Operator-self-trust degradation
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: Without a third-party observer fetching tags, the 'public' part of public timestamp degrades to operator's own claim
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: trust assumption #6 lists this explicitly; multi-observer setup recommended

regress_termination_strategy_inherited: external-out-of-band-trust-anchor (parent cycle's strategy, instantiated here as: git tag with detached signature + push to GitHub remote + recorded in append-only state/meta_cert_anchor_log.jsonl). Formal status: NOT a mathematical termination of the Tarski hierarchy (impossible per parent b2). Epistemic termination via dogmatic-stop at the third-party clock.

omega_stop_witness.method: three files written (workflow + schema + falsifier); each within line budget; sha256 computed and embedded in this witness; 4 axes surfaced (≤4 constraint satisfied); 6-item trust list; SHA-1 limitation explicit with citations; key rotation procedure explicit. Second-pass new-axis discovery 0.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT(weak) — Tier-1 1 / Tier-2 1 / Tier-3 1 / REJECT 0 / Limit-axes 3. All three deliverables sha256-anchored. Trust list ≥5 items satisfied (6 listed). SHA-1 limitation honestly documented as practical-not-theorem. Key-compromise contingency explicit (4-step procedure).
omega_stop_witness.fixpoint_signal: spec triple exists at three named paths with the three sha256 values recorded above; consumer of this witness can re-verify file integrity by `shasum -a 256 <path>` and compare; if mismatch, the witness is falsified per its own terms.

raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_47_strategy_exploration: great-grandchild trawl (meta engine → m3 cert → m3-p5 tarski-verifier → THIS m3-p5-a6 anchor)
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_70_multi_axis_verify: 4 axes: workflow minimality, trust completeness, rotation choice, key-compromise recoverability — each independently falsifiable
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_71_falsifier: spec/m3_anchor_falsifier.md is the falsifier artifact for ALL anchor rows; F1..F5 enumerate refutation conditions; this witness's deliverables are themselves falsifiable by sha256 mismatch
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_77_audit_append_only: anchor log is append-only (schema_version field for evolution); rotation events recorded via notes pointer to last-good-anchor under old keyid; NO history rewrite even after key compromise
raw_strategies_self_applied.self_reference_acknowledgment: this witness JSON does NOT and CANNOT assert the published anchors are honest — it can only specify the protocol whose adherence is verifiable by third parties via the verification command in section 3 of workflow.md.

cross_cycle_coupling.ordinal_relation: ω·2+2 (same as parent — anchor truncates regress at this level, does NOT ascend)
downstream_couplings: tool/anchor_publish.sh (Tier-1 next cycle, ~20 LoC, automates workflow steps 1-5)
test/test_anchor_falsifier.py (validates F1..F5 against synthetic bad anchors)
tool/verify_meta_cert.py (m3.p5 parent — verifier consumes anchor log to bound trust scope)
